tasks.title: Create docker-compose.yml with Jaeger all-in-one service
tasks.description: Initialize Docker Compose infrastructure with Jaeger observability backend as the foundational OTLP trace collection service
tasks.details: Create docker-compose.yml in project root. Add Jaeger all-in-one service using image 'jaegertracing/all-in-one:latest'. Map ports: 16686 (UI), 4317 (OTLP gRPC), 4318 (OTLP HTTP). Set environment variables: SPAN_STORAGE_TYPE=memory, COLLECTOR_OTLP_ENABLED=true, memory.max-traces=50000. Configure restart policy: unless-stopped. Add healthcheck using http://localhost:16686 endpoint with 30s interval, 5s timeout, 3 retries. Add named network 'otel-network' with bridge driver.
tasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose up jaeger' and verify Jaeger UI accessible at http://localhost:16686. Check container logs for successful OTLP receiver startup. Verify ports 4317 and 4318 are listening using 'docker-compose ps' and 'netstat -an | grep 4317'.

subtasks.title: Create docker-compose.yml file in project root
subtasks.description: Initialize the docker-compose.yml file with base structure including version and services declaration
subtasks.details: Create /Users/lucasm/dev/dist-telemetry-exp/docker-compose.yml with Docker Compose version specification (version: '3.8' or higher). Include top-level 'services:' and 'networks:' sections to establish the foundational structure for the multi-service architecture.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate YAML syntax using 'docker-compose config' to ensure the file structure is parsable without errors

subtasks.title: Add Jaeger all-in-one service definition
subtasks.description: Configure the Jaeger service with the jaegertracing/all-in-one:latest image and container naming
subtasks.details: Add 'jaeger' service under 'services:' section. Set image to 'jaegertracing/all-in-one:latest'. Define container_name as 'jaeger' for consistent service discovery. This establishes the core observability backend for OTLP trace collection per PRD requirements.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose config' and verify the jaeger service is properly defined in the parsed configuration output

subtasks.title: Configure Jaeger port mappings for UI and OTLP endpoints
subtasks.description: Map all required Jaeger ports: 16686 (UI), 4317 (OTLP gRPC), 4318 (OTLP HTTP) for trace collection and visualization
subtasks.details: Add 'ports:' section to jaeger service with mappings: '16686:16686' for Jaeger UI access, '4317:4317' for OTLP gRPC receiver (used by .NET services), '4318:4318' for OTLP HTTP receiver. These ports align with the PRD's architecture diagram and service communication requirements.
subtasks.testStrategy: After running 'docker-compose up jaeger -d', verify port bindings using 'docker-compose ps' and 'netstat -an | grep -E "16686|4317|4318"' to confirm all three ports are listening on the host

subtasks.title: Set Jaeger environment variables for OTLP and storage
subtasks.description: Configure SPAN_STORAGE_TYPE, COLLECTOR_OTLP_ENABLED, and memory.max-traces environment variables for in-memory trace storage
subtasks.details: Add 'environment:' section to jaeger service with: SPAN_STORAGE_TYPE=memory (use in-memory storage per PRD), COLLECTOR_OTLP_ENABLED=true (enable OTLP receiver on ports 4317/4318), memory.max-traces=50000 (prevent OOM by limiting trace retention as specified in risk mitigation). These settings enable the OTLP collection pipeline and prevent unbounded memory growth during evaluation sessions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose up jaeger -d' and check container environment variables using 'docker inspect jaeger | grep -A 10 Env'. Verify Jaeger logs show OTLP receiver startup messages with 'docker-compose logs jaeger | grep -i otlp'

subtasks.title: Add healthcheck, restart policy, and otel-network configuration
subtasks.description: Configure healthcheck probe, restart policy, and attach Jaeger to the otel-network bridge network
subtasks.details: Add 'healthcheck:' with test: 'wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:16686 || exit 1', interval: 30s, timeout: 5s, retries: 3. Set 'restart: unless-stopped' to ensure Jaeger survives Docker daemon restarts. Add 'networks: [otel-network]' and define 'networks: otel-network: driver: bridge' at top level. This ensures Jaeger is reliable and accessible to other services (api, orchestrator, machine-simulator) that will be added in future tasks.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose up jaeger' and monitor healthcheck status using 'docker-compose ps' - wait for 'healthy' status (up to 90s). Access Jaeger UI at http://localhost:16686 to verify web interface loads. Verify network creation with 'docker network ls | grep otel-network' and confirm Jaeger is attached with 'docker network inspect otel-network'

tasks.title: Add HiveMQ MQTT broker service to docker-compose.yml
tasks.description: Configure HiveMQ Community Edition MQTT broker service with MQTT 5.0 support for message-based trace context propagation
tasks.details: Add HiveMQ service to docker-compose.yml using image 'hivemq/hivemq-ce:latest'. Map ports: 1883 (MQTT), 8080 (HiveMQ Web UI). Create hivemq-config.xml in ./config/hivemq/ directory enabling anonymous authentication and MQTT 5.0 protocol support. Mount config as volume: './config/hivemq:/opt/hivemq/conf'. Add healthcheck using TCP socket check on port 1883 with 10s interval, 5s timeout, 5 retries. Set restart policy: unless-stopped. Attach to 'otel-network'.
tasks.testStrategy: Start HiveMQ with 'docker-compose up hivemq'. Verify container healthy status. Test MQTT connection using MQTTBox or mosquitto_pub client to localhost:1883. Check HiveMQ Web UI at http://localhost:8080 shows broker running with MQTT 5.0 enabled. Verify config file properly mounted.

subtasks.title: Create config/hivemq directory structure
subtasks.description: Create the ./config/hivemq directory to hold HiveMQ configuration files that will be mounted into the container
subtasks.details: Create the directory structure ./config/hivemq/ in the project root. This directory will be mounted as a volume to /opt/hivemq/conf in the HiveMQ container. The directory needs to exist before docker-compose can mount it as a volume.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify directory exists at ./config/hivemq using 'ls -la config/hivemq'. Ensure proper permissions are set for Docker to mount the directory.

subtasks.title: Create hivemq-config.xml configuration file
subtasks.description: Create the HiveMQ configuration file enabling anonymous authentication and MQTT 5.0 protocol support
subtasks.details: Create ./config/hivemq/hivemq-config.xml file with proper XML structure. Configure anonymous authentication by disabling authentication requirement. Enable MQTT 5.0 protocol support. Set appropriate listeners for MQTT protocol on port 1883. Include typical HiveMQ CE configuration elements like persistence settings and cluster configuration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate XML syntax using xmllint or similar tool. Review configuration to ensure anonymous authentication is enabled and MQTT 5.0 support is configured. Verify file will be properly mounted when HiveMQ container starts.

subtasks.title: Add HiveMQ service definition to docker-compose.yml
subtasks.description: Add the HiveMQ service configuration to docker-compose.yml with image, ports, volumes, and network settings
subtasks.details: Add new 'hivemq' service to docker-compose.yml services section. Use image 'hivemq/hivemq-ce:latest'. Configure ports mapping: '1883:1883' for MQTT protocol and '8080:8080' for HiveMQ Web UI. Mount volume './config/hivemq:/opt/hivemq/conf' for configuration files. Set container_name to 'hivemq' for easier reference. Attach to 'otel-network' network to enable communication with other services like Jaeger and the API.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate docker-compose.yml syntax using 'docker-compose config'. Verify volume path is correctly specified and will mount the hivemq-config.xml file. Check that ports don't conflict with existing services in docker-compose.yml (Jaeger uses 16686, 4317, 4318).

subtasks.title: Add healthcheck configuration to HiveMQ service
subtasks.description: Configure Docker healthcheck for HiveMQ service using TCP socket check on MQTT port 1883
subtasks.details: Add healthcheck section to the hivemq service in docker-compose.yml. Use TCP socket check on port 1883 as the test command: test: ['CMD-SHELL', 'timeout 1 bash -c "</dev/tcp/localhost/1883"' || exit 1]. Set interval: 10s, timeout: 5s, retries: 5. This ensures the MQTT broker is accepting connections before dependent services start. Add restart policy: unless-stopped to match the pattern used by the Jaeger service.
subtasks.testStrategy: Start HiveMQ service with 'docker-compose up -d hivemq'. Monitor healthcheck status using 'docker-compose ps' and verify status transitions from 'starting' to 'healthy'. Check logs with 'docker-compose logs hivemq' for any healthcheck failures. Verify the healthcheck interval and retry settings work as configured.

subtasks.title: Verify HiveMQ service integration and connectivity
subtasks.description: Test the complete HiveMQ service deployment including container health, MQTT connectivity, and Web UI access
subtasks.details:
Start the HiveMQ service using 'docker-compose up hivemq' and verify it reaches healthy status. Test MQTT connection using an MQTT client tool (mosquitto_pub/mosquitto_sub or MQTT Explorer) connecting to localhost:1883. Verify anonymous connections are accepted. Access HiveMQ Web UI at http://localhost:8080 and confirm the broker shows as running with MQTT 5.0 enabled. Check that hivemq-config.xml was properly mounted by inspecting the container filesystem or checking container logs for configuration loading messages.

Create Activity using ActivitySources.LiteDb.StartActivity with name 'litedb.insert MachineReadings' and ActivityKind.Internal. Serialize to JSON using System.Text.Json. Create Activity using ActivitySource.StartActivity('mqtt.publish machines/{machineId}/telemetry', ActivityKind.Producer). Create empty List<MqttUserProperty>, call MqttPropagation.Inject(activity, userProperties). Build MqttApplicationMessage with topic='machines/machine-001/telemetry', payload=UTF8 JSON bytes, UserProperties=userProperties list via .WithUserProperties(). Call client.PublishAsync(message, stoppingToken). Add span attributes: messaging.system='mqtt', messaging.destination='machines/machine-001/telemetry', messaging.operation='publish'. Dispose activity after publish.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run simulator connected to HiveMQ. Check HiveMQ dashboard for messages on 'machines/machine-001/telemetry' topic. Verify message payload is valid JSON with expected MachineReading fields and random values. Use MQTT client (e.g., MQTT Explorer) to subscribe and inspect User Properties contain traceparent and tracestate headers. Verify publish rate matches configured interval (default 5 seconds).

subtasks.title: Configure OpenTelemetry with OTLP exporter and resource attributes in Program.cs
subtasks.description: Set up OpenTelemetry tracing pipeline with Jaeger OTLP exporter, service resource attributes, and ActivitySource registration
subtasks.details: In Program.cs, read otlpEndpoint from IConfiguration (key: 'OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT', default: 'http://jaeger:4317'). Add builder.Services.AddOpenTelemetry().ConfigureResource(resource => resource.AddService(serviceName: 'machine-simulator', serviceVersion: '1.0.0')).WithTracing(tracing => tracing.AddSource('MachineSimulator.MQTT').AddOtlpExporter(options => { options.Endpoint = new Uri(otlpEndpoint); options.Protocol = OtlpExportProtocol.Grpc; }).AddConsoleExporter()). Ensure OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting and OpenTelemetry.Exporter.OpenTelemetryProtocol packages are referenced (already in csproj).
subtasks.testStrategy: Start Jaeger container on localhost:4317. Run simulator and verify OpenTelemetry console exporter shows spans being created. Open Jaeger UI at localhost:16686, search for 'machine-simulator' service, verify spans appear with operation name 'mqtt.publish machines/machine-001/telemetry'. Check span attributes include messaging.system, messaging.destination, messaging.operation. Verify service.name='machine-simulator' in resource attributes.

subtasks.title: Add configuration to appsettings.json and implement graceful shutdown
subtasks.description: Externalize MQTT and OTLP configuration to appsettings.json and implement proper cleanup on CancellationToken cancellation
subtasks.details: Update appsettings.json with sections: 'Mqtt': { 'Host': 'localhost', 'PublishIntervalSeconds': 5 }, 'OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT': 'http://localhost:4317'. In TelemetryPublisher.ExecuteAsync, wrap main loop in try-finally block. In finally block, check if MQTT client is connected and call DisconnectAsync(). Dispose ActivitySource in a class-level Dispose override or using statement. Ensure stoppingToken is passed to Task.Delay and PublishAsync to enable cancellation. Add logging for graceful shutdown: 'Disconnecting MQTT client', 'TelemetryPublisher stopped'. Test shutdown behavior.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run simulator with configured appsettings.json values. Verify MQTT host and publish interval are read correctly. Send SIGTERM or Ctrl+C, verify graceful shutdown logs appear. Check MQTT client disconnects cleanly (HiveMQ dashboard shows client disconnect). Verify no resource leaks or exceptions during shutdown. Test changing publishIntervalSeconds in config and confirm new interval is used on restart.

tasks.title: Create multi-stage Dockerfile for Machine Simulator
tasks.description: Build optimized Docker container for machine-simulator Worker Service with .NET 8 runtime
tasks.details: Create src/MachineSimulator/Dockerfile. Stage 1 (build): FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0, WORKDIR /src, COPY MachineSimulator.csproj, RUN dotnet restore, COPY source, RUN dotnet publish -c Release -o /app/publish. Stage 2 (runtime): FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime:8.0, WORKDIR /app, COPY from build, use non-root user, ENTRYPOINT dotnet MachineSimulator.dll. Image should be <100MB.
tasks.testStrategy: Build with 'docker build -t machine-simulator:latest src/MachineSimulator'. Verify image builds successfully. Run standalone container with environment variables for MQTT host and OTLP endpoint. Check logs for successful MQTT connection and message publishing. Validate container stops gracefully on SIGTERM.

tasks.title: Add Machine Simulator service to docker-compose.yml
tasks.description: Integrate machine-simulator into Docker Compose with HiveMQ and Jaeger dependencies and environment-based configuration
tasks.details: Add 'machine-simulator' service to docker-compose.yml. Set build context './src/MachineSimulator'. Set environment variables: MQTT_HOST=hivemq, MQTT_PORT=1883, PUBLISH_INTERVAL_SECONDS=5, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://jaeger:4317, OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=machine-simulator, OTEL_SERVICE_VERSION=1.0.0, MACHINE_ID=machine-001. Set depends_on: hivemq (service_started), jaeger (service_started). Attach to 'otel-network'. Set restart: unless-stopped. No ports need mapping (outbound client only).
tasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose up machine-simulator'. Verify container starts after HiveMQ and Jaeger. Check logs show MQTT connection success and periodic publish messages. Use MQTT client to subscribe to 'machines/+/telemetry' and verify messages received. Check Jaeger UI for machine-simulator traces. Verify messages contain User Properties with traceparent header.

tasks.title: Create .NET 8 Worker Service project for Orchestrator

Configure project for Docker. Remove default Worker. Enable nullable reference types.
tasks.testStrategy: Build project with 'dotnet build src/Orchestrator'. Verify all packages restore. Check project references HttpClient factory and MQTT client libraries correctly. Run standalone to verify Worker Service host initializes.

subtasks.title: Add NuGet package references for MQTT, HTTP, and OpenTelemetry

tasks.title: Implement MQTT subscriber with trace context extraction in Orchestrator

In message received handler: extract parent context using Propagators.DefaultTextMapPropagator.Extract with MqttUserPropertiesGetter over e.ApplicationMessage.UserProperties. Create Activity named 'mqtt.process machines/{id}/telemetry' with ActivityKind.Consumer and parentContext.ActivityContext as parent. Add attributes: messaging.system='mqtt', messaging.destination=topic, messaging.operation='process'. Deserialize JSON payload to MachineReading. Pass to HTTP client for API forwarding. Dispose Activity when processing complete.

Check Activity attributes present.

Implement reconnection logic from TelemetryPublisher.cs:30-48 using DisconnectedAsync event handler. Implement initial connection with retry logic (lines 50-66 pattern). Read mqttHost from configuration["Mqtt:Host"] ?? "localhost". Implement graceful shutdown in StopAsync disposing client.

subtasks.title: Implement MQTT subscription to machines/+/telemetry topic
subtasks.description: Subscribe to wildcard topic filter with QoS AtLeastOnce and wire up message received event handler
subtasks.details: After successful MQTT connection in TelemetryProcessor.ExecuteAsync, call _mqttClient.SubscribeAsync with MqttClientSubscribeOptionsBuilder. Configure topic filter 'machines/+/telemetry' with QoS AtLeastOnce. Wire up _mqttClient.ApplicationMessageReceivedAsync event handler (async lambda receiving MqttApplicationMessageReceivedEventArgs e). Log subscription success with topic filter. In message handler, log received message with topic e.ApplicationMessage.Topic. Add try-catch around handler to prevent unhandled exceptions. Use logger.LogError for any exceptions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run Orchestrator with MachineSimulator publishing. Verify logs show 'Subscribed to machines/+/telemetry'. Confirm message received logs appear with topic 'machines/machine-001/telemetry'. Verify wildcard matching by publishing to machines/machine-002/telemetry.

subtasks.title: Extract trace context and create linked Consumer Activity in message handler
subtasks.description: Implement W3C trace context extraction from MQTT User Properties and create child Activity with proper parent linking
subtasks.details: In ApplicationMessageReceivedAsync handler, extract parent context using MqttPropagation.Extract(e.ApplicationMessage.UserProperties). Create Activity using ActivitySource.StartActivity with name 'mqtt.process ' + e.ApplicationMessage.Topic, ActivityKind.Consumer, and parentContext: parentContext.ActivityContext. Set attributes: messaging.system='mqtt', messaging.destination=e.ApplicationMessage.Topic, messaging.operation='process'. Deserialize e.ApplicationMessage.PayloadSegment using JsonSerializer.Deserialize<MachineReading>(). Log deserialized reading with machine ID and values. Wrap Activity in using statement for automatic disposal. Log extracted TraceId and SpanId at debug level. For now, just log the deserialized MachineReading (HTTP forwarding is Task 15).
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full stack: Jaeger, HiveMQ, TelemetryApi, MachineSimulator, Orchestrator. In Jaeger UI, find trace starting from MachineSimulator 'mqtt.publish' span. Verify Orchestrator 'mqtt.process machines/machine-001/telemetry' span appears as child with matching TraceId. Verify parent-child relationship shows in trace timeline. Check span attributes match OTel semantic conventions. Verify MachineReading deserialization in logs.

tasks.title: Implement typed HttpClient for API communication with auto-instrumentation
tasks.description: Configure HttpClient factory with OpenTelemetry instrumentation to forward telemetry data to API service
tasks.details: In Program.cs, register typed HttpClient: builder.Services.AddHttpClient<ITelemetryApiClient, TelemetryApiClient>(client => { client.BaseAddress = new Uri(apiBaseUrl); client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Add('Accept', 'application/json'); }). Create Clients/TelemetryApiClient.cs with PostReadingAsync method accepting MachineReading, serializing to JSON, POSTing to '/api/readings'. Configure OpenTelemetry tracing with .WithTracing(builder => builder.AddHttpClientInstrumentation().AddOtlpExporter()). Set ResourceBuilder with service.name='orchestrator'. HttpClient spans automatically created by OTel instrumentation and linked to parent MQTT processing Activity.
tasks.testStrategy: Run full stack (Jaeger, HiveMQ, API, machine-simulator, orchestrator). Verify orchestrator receives MQTT messages and forwards to API. Check API logs for POST requests. Verify data appears in LiteDB via GET /api/readings. Critically: open Jaeger UI, find trace starting in machine-simulator, verify single trace spanning all 4 spans: simulator publish → orchestrator process → orchestrator HTTP client → api ASP.NET Core → api LiteDB. Validate parent-child relationships correct.

subtasks.title: Create ITelemetryApiClient interface and TelemetryApiClient implementation
subtasks.description: Create Clients/ITelemetryApiClient.cs interface and Clients/TelemetryApiClient.cs typed HttpClient implementation with PostReadingAsync method
subtasks.details: Create src/Orchestrator/Clients/ directory. Define ITelemetryApiClient interface with Task<HttpResponseMessage> PostReadingAsync(MachineReading reading, CancellationToken ct). Implement TelemetryApiClient class with constructor accepting HttpClient and ILogger. In PostReadingAsync: transform Orchestrator.Models.MachineReading (Temperature, Pressure, Rpm) into three separate API MachineReading objects (Metric='Temperature', Value=reading.Temperature, Unit='C'), serialize to JSON with JsonSerializer, POST to '/api/readings' endpoint, return response. Include Activity.Current?.Id as TraceId in payload.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test TelemetryApiClient with mocked HttpClient using HttpMessageHandler. Verify JSON payload structure, endpoint URL '/api/readings', Accept header 'application/json'. Verify three POST requests generated per MachineReading (one per metric). Mock successful 201 response and verify method completes without exceptions.

subtasks.title: Configure OpenTelemetry with HttpClient instrumentation and OTLP exporter in Program.cs
subtasks.description: Add OpenTelemetry tracing pipeline to Program.cs with HttpClient instrumentation, ActivitySource registration, and OTLP exporter configuration
subtasks.details: In Program.cs, add builder.Services.AddOpenTelemetry().WithTracing(tracerProvider => tracerProvider.AddSource('Orchestrator.MQTT').AddHttpClientInstrumentation().AddOtlpExporter(options => { options.Endpoint = new Uri(builder.Configuration['Otel:OtlpEndpoint'] ?? 'http://localhost:4317'); })).ConfigureResource(resource => resource.AddService('orchestrator', serviceVersion: '1.0.0')). This enables automatic span creation for all HttpClient requests with parent-child linking to MQTT processing Activity from TelemetryProcessor line 58.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run orchestrator with Jaeger backend. Verify 'orchestrator' service appears in Jaeger UI. Create test that triggers HTTP request, verify span named 'HTTP POST' appears as child of parent Activity. Check span attributes include http.method=POST, http.url, http.status_code. Verify OTLP exporter connects to configured endpoint without errors in logs.

subtasks.title: Register typed HttpClient with base address and default headers in Program.cs
subtasks.description: Configure HttpClient factory in Program.cs to register TelemetryApiClient with base address from configuration and Accept header
subtasks.details: In Program.cs after OpenTelemetry setup, add: var apiBaseUrl = builder.Configuration['Api:BaseUrl'] ?? 'http://localhost:5000'; builder.Services.AddHttpClient<ITelemetryApiClient, TelemetryApiClient>(client => { client.BaseAddress = new Uri(apiBaseUrl); client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Add('Accept', 'application/json'); client.Timeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(30); }). This registers typed client with DI, enabling constructor injection into TelemetryProcessor.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test with TestServer hosting minimal API endpoint. Register HttpClient with test base URL. Inject ITelemetryApiClient via DI, call PostReadingAsync, verify request reaches test endpoint with correct BaseAddress and Accept header. Verify HttpClient timeout is 30 seconds. Test DI resolution of ITelemetryApiClient returns TelemetryApiClient instance.

This registers the existing TelemetryProcessor BackgroundService with the host, ensuring it starts when the application runs. TelemetryProcessor already exists at src/Orchestrator/Services/TelemetryProcessor.cs with MQTT client setup and message handling logic. Registration enables DI of ITelemetryApiClient into constructor.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run orchestrator with 'dotnet run'. Verify TelemetryProcessor logs appear: 'TelemetryProcessor starting', 'MQTT connected to localhost', 'Subscribed to machines/+/telemetry'. Test DI by injecting ITelemetryApiClient into TelemetryProcessor constructor, verify no DI resolution errors. Stop application with Ctrl+C, verify 'TelemetryProcessor stopped' log appears and graceful shutdown occurs.

subtasks.title: Integrate TelemetryApiClient into TelemetryProcessor HTTP forwarding logic
subtasks.description: Wire ITelemetryApiClient into TelemetryProcessor message handler to replace placeholder comment with actual HTTP forwarding call

Current Activity propagates TraceId to API via OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Full integration test: run Jaeger (port 16686), HiveMQ (port 1883), TelemetryApi (port 5000), MachineSimulator, and Orchestrator. Open Jaeger UI, search for 'orchestrator' service, verify trace shows: mqtt.process span -> HTTP POST span -> ASP.NET Core span -> litedb.insert span in single distributed trace.

subtasks.details: In src/TelemetryApi/Program.cs after var app = builder.Build() (line 33), use ILogger to log configuration values: LiteDb:ConnectionString, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, and app.Urls. Log at Information level showing 'TelemetryApi starting with: LiteDb={path}, OTLP={endpoint}, URLs={urls}'. Do NOT log any secrets. Add validation that LiteDb:ConnectionString is not null/empty, throw clear exception if missing critical config.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run dotnet run in TelemetryApi directory and verify startup logs display: 'TelemetryApi starting with: LiteDb=./data/telemetry.db, OTLP=http://localhost:4317, URLs=http://localhost:5000'. Override with environment variables (LiteDb__ConnectionString=/tmp/test.db) and verify log shows overridden value. Test validation by removing LiteDb config and verify startup fails with clear error message.

subtasks.title: Implement configuration validation and startup logging in MachineSimulator and Orchestrator
subtasks.description: Add startup logging in MachineSimulator and Orchestrator Program.cs files to display actual configuration values loaded (MQTT host, API URL, OTLP endpoint, publish interval) with validation
subtasks.details: In src/MachineSimulator/Program.cs after var host = builder.Build(), log 'MachineSimulator starting with: Mqtt:Host={host}, PublishInterval={seconds}s, OTLP={endpoint}'. In src/Orchestrator/Program.cs after var host = builder.Build() (line 34), log 'Orchestrator starting with: Mqtt:Host={host}, Api:BaseUrl={url}, OTLP={endpoint}'. Use ILogger at Information level. Add validation that Mqtt:Host and Api:BaseUrl are not null/empty for Orchestrator, throw clear exception if missing. Do NOT log secrets.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run dotnet run for both MachineSimulator and Orchestrator. Verify startup logs show configuration values loaded from appsettings.json. Override Mqtt__Host environment variable and verify logs show overridden value. Test with docker-compose up and verify logs show container-specific values (Mqtt__Host=hivemq, Api__BaseUrl=http://telemetry-api:8080) matching docker-compose.yml environment blocks.

tasks.title: Add structured logging with trace correlation across all services
tasks.description: Configure Microsoft.Extensions.Logging with trace ID correlation and structured output for debugging and production monitoring
tasks.details: In each service Program.cs, configure logging: builder.Logging.ClearProviders().AddConsole().AddJsonConsole(options => options.IncludeScopes = true). In API, add middleware to inject TraceId into log scope using Activity.Current?.TraceId. In Worker Services, log TraceId in message handlers. Use structured logging with message templates: _logger.LogInformation('Processing message for {MachineId} with TraceId {TraceId}', machineId, Activity.Current?.TraceId). Configure minimum log levels via appsettings.json: Information for production, Debug for development. Add correlation ID to HTTP response headers in API.
tasks.testStrategy: Run full stack and examine logs from each container. Verify log entries include TraceId matching spans in Jaeger. Grep logs for a specific TraceId and verify all related log entries across all three services found. Test different log levels. Verify structured JSON format when configured. Check API response headers include X-Correlation-Id.

tasks.title: Implement graceful shutdown with CancellationToken propagation
tasks.description: Add proper shutdown handling in all Worker Services to ensure clean MQTT disconnection and activity disposal
tasks.details: In MachineSimulator TelemetryPublisher.ExecuteAsync, pass stoppingToken to Task.Delay and MQTT client operations. On cancellation, disconnect MQTT client gracefully, dispose ActivitySource. In Orchestrator TelemetryProcessor, on shutdown unsubscribe from MQTT topics, disconnect client, dispose HttpClient and ActivitySource. Add try-catch-finally blocks ensuring cleanup. Configure WorkerOptions in Program.cs with ShutdownTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(30). Test with docker-compose stop and verify logs show 'Application is shutting down' and clean disconnection messages.
tasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose up', wait for steady state, then 'docker-compose stop'. Verify all containers stop within 30 seconds without force kill. Check logs show graceful shutdown messages: MQTT disconnected, activities flushed to OTLP. Restart with 'docker-compose start' and verify clean reconnection. Test SIGTERM handling with 'docker kill -s TERM <container>'.

tasks.title: Add docker-compose.override.yml for local development
tasks.description: Create override file enabling volume mounts for hot reload and local debugging with source code
tasks.details: Create docker-compose.override.yml. For api service: mount './src/TelemetryApi:/app' as volume, set ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Development, add port mapping for debugger 5001:5001. For machine-simulator and orchestrator: mount source directories, set Development environment. Add comments explaining override purpose. Document in README that override is automatically applied with docker-compose up. Add .dockerignore files in each project excluding bin/, obj/, .git/ to optimize build context.
tasks.testStrategy: With override file present, run docker-compose up --build. Modify source file (e.g., change log message). Verify change reflected without full rebuild (for projects supporting hot reload). Remove override file and verify production build still works. Test .dockerignore by checking Docker build context size in build output.

tasks.title: Finalize docker-compose.yml with resource limits and restart policies
tasks.description: Add production-ready resource constraints, restart policies, and named volumes to docker-compose.yml
tasks.details: For each service in docker-compose.yml, add deploy.resources.limits: memory: 512M (1G for Jaeger), cpus: '0.5'. Set restart: unless-stopped for all services. Create named volumes: litedb-data (already exists), jaeger-data (if using Badger storage). Add labels for documentation: 'com.example.description', 'com.example.version'. Configure networks section explicitly with 'otel-network' bridge driver, enable_ipv6: false. Add container_name for each service for easier reference. Review all healthchecks are present and tuned appropriately.
tasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose config' and verify output is valid YAML. Run 'docker-compose up' and verify resource limits applied using 'docker stats'. Kill a container and verify restart policy brings it back. Test with 'docker-compose down -v' to remove volumes, then 'docker-compose up' to verify clean state initialization. Check container names match expected values.

tasks.title: Create comprehensive README.md with setup and validation instructions
tasks.description: Write complete documentation covering architecture, setup, validation steps, Jaeger navigation, and troubleshooting
tasks.details: Create README.md with sections: Overview (project purpose and architecture diagram reference), Prerequisites (Docker, Compose version requirements), Quick Start (docker-compose up single command), Architecture (service descriptions, data flow, trace flow diagrams), Validation Steps (how to verify each component working: Jaeger UI, HiveMQ, API endpoints, end-to-end trace), Jaeger UI Navigation (how to find traces, interpret span waterfall, inspect attributes), Expected Trace Structure (annotated description of full trace with all spans and attributes), Configuration (environment variables table), Troubleshooting (common issues: port conflicts, MQTT version, OTLP connection failures), Development (how to use override, rebuild, local debugging), Technology Stack (table of libraries and versions), Future Enhancements (Phase 6 items). Include annotated curl commands for testing API. Add screenshots or ASCII diagrams of expected Jaeger trace.
tasks.testStrategy: Follow README from clean state as new user would. Verify every command works. Check all links valid. Validate curl examples return expected responses. Have another developer follow README and provide feedback. Ensure troubleshooting section covers issues encountered during development.

tasks.title: Create unit tests for MQTT trace context propagation helpers
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests validating W3C TraceContext injection and extraction correctness
tasks.details: Create test projects: MachineSimulator.Tests, Orchestrator.Tests. Add xunit, FluentAssertions, Moq packages. Test MqttUserPropertiesSetter: create Activity with known TraceId and SpanId, inject into empty list, assert traceparent format matches spec, verify tracestate if present. Test MqttUserPropertiesGetter: populate list with valid traceparent, extract, assert ActivityContext has correct TraceId/SpanId/TraceFlags. Test edge cases: missing traceparent (returns default context), malformed traceparent (returns default), multiple traceparent values (uses first). Test baggage propagation if used. Achieve >90% code coverage for propagation helpers.
tasks.testStrategy: Run 'dotnet test' for all test projects. Verify all tests pass. Check code coverage report. Test mutation testing with Stryker.NET to verify test quality. Run tests in CI/CD pipeline. Validate tests catch regressions when propagation code modified incorrectly.

tasks.title: Add integration test validating end-to-end trace propagation
tasks.description: Create automated integration test that verifies complete trace chain from simulator through orchestrator to API appears as single trace in Jaeger
tasks.details: Create IntegrationTests project with docker-compose test profile. Use TestContainers or Docker.DotNet to manage test environment lifecycle. Test scenario: start all services, wait for healthy state, trigger simulator publish (or inject test message), wait for API persistence, query Jaeger API (http://localhost:16686/api/traces) searching for traces with service 'machine-simulator', find most recent trace, validate trace structure: root span is publish span with service=machine-simulator, child span is process span with service=orchestrator, grandchild is HTTP client span, great-grandchild is ASP.NET Core span with service=telemetry-api, great-great-grandchild is LiteDB span. Assert all spans share same TraceId. Assert parent-child relationships correct using span.references. Validate span attributes present (messaging.*, db.*, http.*).
tasks.testStrategy: Run integration test suite with 'dotnet test IntegrationTests'. Verify test completes in <60 seconds. Check test reliably passes with clean environment. Test failure cases: if orchestrator not running, trace incomplete (only simulator span). Verify test cleanup tears down containers. Run in CI environment to validate reproducibility.

tasks.title: Replace HealthController with ASP.NET Core Health Checks
tasks.description: Remove the custom HealthController and replace it with the built-in ASP.NET Core Health Checks framework
tasks.details: Remove src/TelemetryApi/Controllers/HealthController.cs. In Program.cs, add builder.Services.AddHealthChecks() and register any relevant checks (e.g., a custom LiteDB connectivity check). Map the health endpoint with app.MapHealthChecks("/health"). Optionally configure a custom response writer using HealthCheckOptions to return a JSON payload matching the existing response shape: { status, timestamp, service, version }. The LiteDB health check should attempt a lightweight query to verify the database is accessible.
tasks.testStrategy: Call GET /health and verify 200 OK with healthy status JSON. Simulate a LiteDB failure and verify the endpoint returns 503 Unhealthy. Confirm the endpoint is registered via app.MapHealthChecks and not via a controller action.
